ferryPage.title: Ferry Tickets to Greek Islands
ferryPage.subtitle: Book your ferry crossing from Bodrum, Turgutreis or Marmaris to Kos, Rhodes, Samos and beyond.
ferryPage.heroImageAlt: Ferry sailing in the Aegean Sea
ferryPage.popularRoutesSubtitle: Direct connections from Turkish coast to Greek islands
ferryPage.whyTitle: Why Book Ferry Tickets With Us?
ferryPage.whySubtitle: The trusted choice for Turkish travelers to Greek islands
ferryPage.why1Desc: We compare all ferry operators to offer you the best available prices. No hidden fees.
ferryPage.why2Desc: Our office in Kos Port means we know every route, schedule, and operator firsthand.
ferryPage.why3Desc: Our Turkish-speaking team assists you before, during, and after your journey.
ferryPage.faqSubtitle: Everything you need to know about ferry travel
ferryPage.faq1Q: What documents do I need for the ferry crossing?
ferryPage.faq1A: You need a valid passport (at least 6 months validity). Turkish citizens traveling to Greece for tourism (up to 90 days in 180 days) may need a Schengen visa depending on current regulations. We recommend checking the latest requirements before booking.
ferryPage.faq2Q: Can I bring my car on the ferry?
ferryPage.faq2A: Most ferries between Turkey and the Greek islands are passenger-only. If you need a vehicle on the island, we recommend booking a car rental with us - we offer port pickup service in Kos.
ferryPage.faq3A: Generally, you can bring hand luggage and 1-2 suitcases per person. There are no strict weight limits, but large or excessive luggage may incur additional fees. Contact us for specific ferry operator policies.
ferryPage.faq4Q: Can I change my ferry ticket date?
ferryPage.faq4A: Date changes can be requested up to 48 hours before departure, subject to availability. Tickets are non-refundable and cannot be cancelled. We recommend travel insurance to cover unforeseen circumstances.
ferryPage.faq5Q: Is the ferry comfortable for families with children?
ferryPage.faq5A: Yes! Ferries have comfortable seating, air conditioning, and snack bars. The journey is relatively short (1-3 hours depending on route). Children under 4 often travel free or at reduced rates.
ferryPage.faq6A: Yes, we offer both one-way and round-trip tickets. Round-trip tickets are typically 10-15% cheaper than booking two one-way tickets separately.
ferryPage.ctaDescription: Our team speaks Turkish and can help you find the perfect ferry schedule and best prices.

carRentalPage.title: Car Rental in Kos
carRentalPage.subtitle: Rent a car directly at Kos Port. Same-day pickup, full insurance, English & Turkish support.
carRentalPage.fleetSubtitle: Well-maintained vehicles perfect for exploring Kos Island
carRentalPage.emptyDesc: We couldn't find any cars at the moment. Please try again later or contact us directly.
carRentalPage.exploreDesc: Beautiful beaches, ancient ruins, and charming villages await
carRentalPage.faqSubtitle: Everything you need to know about car rental in Kos
carRentalPage.faq1Q: What documents do I need to rent a car in Kos?
carRentalPage.faq1A: You need a valid driving license (held for at least 1 year), passport or ID, and a credit/debit card. International Driving Permits (IDP) are recommended but not required for EU and Turkish licenses.
carRentalPage.faq2Q: Can you deliver the car to the port when I arrive?
carRentalPage.faq2A: Yes! Port delivery is included free of charge. We will meet you at Kos Port with your car ready and waiting. Just let us know your ferry arrival time.
carRentalPage.faq3Q: Is insurance included in the price?
carRentalPage.faq3A: Yes, all rentals include basic insurance (CDW - Collision Damage Waiver and TPL - Third Party Liability). We also offer full coverage upgrades that eliminate your excess for complete peace of mind.
carRentalPage.faq4A: We operate a "full to full" policy. You receive the car with a full tank and return it with a full tank. Fuel stations are easy to find throughout Kos.
carRentalPage.faq5Q: Can I drive to other islands with the rental car?
carRentalPage.faq5A: Our cars are only authorized for use on Kos Island. If you want to visit other islands, we recommend taking a ferry as a foot passenger or booking one of our island-hopping tours.
carRentalPage.faq6Q: What is the minimum age to rent a car?
carRentalPage.faq6A: The minimum age is 21 years old, and you must have held your driving license for at least 1 year. Drivers under 25 may have a young driver surcharge depending on the vehicle category.
carRentalPage.ctaDescription: Our team can recommend the perfect car for your trip and answer any questions in Turkish.

hero.title: Tours & Excursions
hero.subtitle: Discover the magic of the Greek islands with our curated tours. Boat trips, cultural excursions, sunset sailing, and island-hopping adventures led by local experts.

t1.name: Three Islands Cruise
t1.desc: Visit three stunning islands in one day. Swim in crystal-clear waters, explore traditional villages, and enjoy a Greek lunch on board.

t2.name: Rhodes Old Town Day Trip
t2.desc: Explore the UNESCO World Heritage medieval old town of Rhodes, the Palace of the Grand Master, and the famous Street of Knights.

t3.name: Sunset Sailing Adventure
t3.desc: Sail along the Kos coastline as the sun sets over the Aegean. Includes swimming, snorkeling, and champagne toast.

t4.name: Ancient Asklepion & Wine Tour
t4.desc: Visit the ancient healing temple of Asklepion where Hippocrates taught medicine, followed by wine tasting at a local vineyard.

t5.name: Nisyros Volcano Excursion
t5.desc: Journey to the volcanic island of Nisyros. Walk into the crater of an active volcano and explore the picturesque village of Mandraki.

t6.name: Beach Hopping BBQ Cruise
t6.desc: Cruise to Kos hidden beaches accessible only by boat. Swim in turquoise waters and enjoy a traditional Greek BBQ lunch.

why.title: Why Book Tours With Us?
why.intro: Creating unforgettable experiences in the Greek islands
why.c1Desc: Intimate tours with maximum 15 people for a personal experience
why.c2Desc: Guides who know every hidden gem and local story
why.c3Desc: Free cancellation up to 24 hours before departure
why.c4Desc: Full Turkish language support before and during your tour

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.intro: Everything you need to know about our tours
faq.q1: How do I book a tour?
faq.a1: You can book directly through our website, via WhatsApp, or by visiting our office at Kos Port. We recommend booking at least 24-48 hours in advance, especially during peak season (June-August).
faq.q2: What should I bring on a boat tour?
faq.a2: We recommend bringing sunscreen, sunglasses, a hat, swimwear, a towel, comfortable shoes for walking, and a camera. Most tours provide lunch and drinks, but you may bring snacks. Cash for souvenirs is also useful.
faq.q3: Are the tours suitable for children?
faq.a3: Most of our tours are family-friendly! Children under 4 often travel free, and ages 4-12 receive discounted rates. The Three Islands Cruise and Beach BBQ are particularly popular with families. Let us know the ages of your children when booking.
faq.q4: What happens if weather is bad?
faq.a4: Safety is our priority. If a tour is cancelled due to weather, you can choose a full refund or reschedule to another date at no extra cost. We monitor weather conditions closely and will contact you if changes are needed.
faq.q5: Do I need a visa for island-hopping tours?
faq.a5: If you already have a valid Schengen visa or are in Greece legally, no additional visa is needed for tours to other Greek islands. For day trips departing from Kos, your existing entry stamp is sufficient.
faq.a6: Most full-day tours include lunch and beverages. Half-day tours typically include snacks and water. Check the specific tour details for what is included. Vegetarian and special dietary options are available with advance notice.

cta.title: Need Help Choosing a Tour?
cta.desc: Our team can recommend the perfect experience based on your interests, group size, and travel dates.

hiw.intro: Simple 4-step process to receive your international packages in Kos
hiw.step1Desc: Contact us via WhatsApp to reserve your package slot or storage box.
hiw.step2Desc: Ship your online purchases to our secure Kos Port office address.
hiw.step3Desc: We notify you via WhatsApp when your package arrives safely.
hiw.step4Desc: Collect your packages at Kos Port before returning to Turkey.

notice.title: Important Notice
notice.body: Customers are responsible for customs duties, taxes, product legality, and all import/export regulations. TravelBeez provides address, storage, and handover services only. We do not inspect package contents and cannot be held responsible for the nature of items shipped. Please ensure your purchases comply with both Greek and Turkish customs regulations.
